mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, labor market slack signals severe demand destruction and recessionary conditions, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) after 2010.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20d returns (accuracy < 50%), or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indexes signal strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during post-recession recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5d returns during recovery periods (Rank IC < 0.01), or the mechanism reverses direction during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy and erodes real corporate margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 45% in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and real GDP confirms broad-based economic expansion and rising household income, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and GDPC1 yields negative 60d returns in > 50% of quarters, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index reflect weakening manufacturing activity and reduced corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes fail to predict negative 20d returns (accuracy < 45%), or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 700,000 units signal robust construction demand and leveraged economic activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in > 55% of months where HOUST surpasses 700.0, or the threshold signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.015) after 2011.
